In [38]:
from texttable import Texttable

# Initialize the table
table = Texttable()

# Define the table headers and rows
table.header(["Task", "Sub-task", "Comments"])
table.add_rows([
    ["Data Preprocessing", "Scaling and Resizing", "Done"],
    ["", "Image Augmentation", "Done"],
    ["", "Train and test data handled correctly", "Done"],
    ["", "Gaussian Blur, Histogram Equalization and Intensity thresholds", "Not Done"],
    
    ["Model Training", "Training Time?", "Initial training- 144.8 minutes, Fine tuning-49.3 minutes"],
    ["", "AUC and Confusion Matrix Computed", "Done"],
    ["", "Overfitting/Underfitting checked and handled", "Done"],
    
    ["Empirical Tuning", "Interpretability Implemented", "The confusion matrices show that most predictions are correct, with relatively few misclassifications"],
    ["", "1st Round of Tuning", "Took abit of time but performed at optimum at 6 out of 20 epochs"],
    ["", "2nd Round of Tuning", "settled for 4 epochs because of the 1st tuning"],
])

# Print the table
print(table.draw())

+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
| Data Preprocessing |    Scaling and Resizing    |            Done            |
+====================+============================+============================+
|                    | Image Augmentation         | Done                       |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
|                    | Train and test data        | Done                       |
|                    | handled correctly          |                            |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
|                    | Gaussian Blur, Histogram   | Not Done                   |
|                    | Equalization and Intensity |                            |
|                    | thresholds                 |                            |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
| Model Training     | Training Time?             | Initial training-          |
|                    |                            | 144.8 minutes, Fine        |
|                    |                            | tuning-49.3 minutes        |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
|                    | AUC and Confusion Matrix   | Done                       |
|                    | Computed                   |                            |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
|                    | Overfitting/Underfitting   | Done                       |
|                    | checked and handled        |                            |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
| Empirical Tuning   | Interpretability           | The confusion matrices     |
|                    | Implemented                | show that most predictions |
|                    |                            | are correct, with          |
|                    |                            | relatively few             |
|                    |                            | misclassifications         |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
|                    | 1st Round of Tuning        | Took abit of time but      |
|                    |                            | performed at optimum at 6  |
|                    |                            | out of 20 epochs           |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+
|                    | 2nd Round of Tuning        | settled for 4 epochs       |
|                    |                            | because of the 1st tuning  |
+--------------------+----------------------------+----------------------------+

Import Libraries¶

In [10]:
import os
import pickle
import numpy as np
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.keras.applications import EfficientNetB0
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense, GlobalAveragePooling2D, Dropout
from tensorflow.keras.models import Model
from tensorflow.keras.optimizers import Adam
from tensorflow.keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping, ReduceLROnPlateau
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, roc_auc_score, RocCurveDisplay
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
import json
from PIL import Image
from collections import Counter
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

Load file paths¶

In [2]:
json_file_path = r'C:\Users\COMFORT\Downloads\Yelp-Photos\Yelp Photos\yelp_photos\photos.json'
image_folder = r'C:\Users\COMFORT\Downloads\Yelp-Photos\Yelp Photos\yelp_photos\photos'
output_pickle_path = r'C:\Users\COMFORT\Downloads\Yelp-Photos\image_label_mapping_20000.pkl'

Verify JSON File Loading¶

In [3]:
print("Verifying JSON file loading...")
with open(json_file_path, "r") as f:
    sample_lines = [next(f) for _ in range(5)]  # Read the first 5 lines
for line in sample_lines:
    print(line.strip())  # Print each line to verify the structure

{"photo_id": "zsvj7vloL4L5jhYyPIuVwg", "business_id": "Nk-SJhPlDBkAZvfsADtccA", "caption": "Nice rock artwork everywhere and craploads of taps.", "label": "inside"}
{"photo_id": "HCUdRJHHm_e0OCTlZetGLg", "business_id": "yVZtL5MmrpiivyCIrVkGgA", "caption": "", "label": "outside"}
{"photo_id": "vkr8T0scuJmGVvN2HJelEA", "business_id": "_ab50qdWOk0DdB6XOrBitw", "caption": "oyster shooter", "label": "drink"}
{"photo_id": "pve7D6NUrafHW3EAORubyw", "business_id": "SZU9c8V2GuREDN5KgyHFJw", "caption": "Shrimp scampi", "label": "food"}
{"photo_id": "H52Er-uBg6rNrHcReWTD2w", "business_id": "Gzur0f0XMkrVxIwYJvOt2g", "caption": "", "label": "food"}

Map Images to Labels and Remove Corrupt Images¶

In [4]:
image_label_mapping = {}
valid_images_count = 0
max_images = 20000

print("\nMapping images to labels and checking for corrupt images...")
with open(json_file_path, "r") as f:
    for line in f:
        try:
            entry = json.loads(line.strip())
            image_id = entry["photo_id"] + ".jpg"
            label = entry["label"]
            image_path = os.path.join(image_folder, image_id)

            # Check if the image file exists and is not corrupt
            if os.path.exists(image_path):
                try:
                    # Attempt to open the image to check if it's valid
                    with Image.open(image_path) as img:
                        img.verify()  # Verify that the file is not corrupt
                    image_label_mapping[image_id] = label
                    valid_images_count += 1

                    # Print the first few valid images for verification
                    if valid_images_count <= 5:
                        print(f"Valid image: {image_id}, Label: {label}")

                    # Stop if we reach the limit of 20,000 valid images
                    if valid_images_count >= max_images:
                        break
                except (IOError, SyntaxError):
                    # Skip corrupt images
                    print(f"Skipping corrupt image: {image_id}")
                    continue
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            print(f"Error parsing JSON line: {e}")

print(f"\nTotal valid images found: {len(image_label_mapping)}")

Save the Mapping to a Pickle File¶

In [5]:
with open(output_pickle_path, "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(image_label_mapping, f)

print(f"✅ Saved {len(image_label_mapping)} valid image-label mappings to {output_pickle_path}")

✅ Saved 20000 valid image-label mappings to C:\Users\COMFORT\Downloads\Yelp-Photos\image_label_mapping_20000.pkl

Load the Image-Label Mapping¶

In [6]:
with open(output_pickle_path, "rb") as f:
    image_label_mapping = pickle.load(f)

Extract Image Paths and Labels¶

In [7]:
image_paths = []
labels = []
for image_id, label in image_label_mapping.items():
    image_path = os.path.join(image_folder, image_id)
    image_paths.append(image_path)
    labels.append(label)

Perform Label Encoding¶

In [8]:
label_encoder = LabelEncoder()
labels_encoded = label_encoder.fit_transform(labels)  # Convert labels to integers
num_classes = len(label_encoder.classes_)  # Get the number of unique classes

Check the class distribution¶

In [11]:
print("EDA: Class Distribution")
label_counts = Counter(labels)
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.bar(label_counts.keys(), label_counts.values())
plt.title("Class Distribution")
plt.xlabel("Class Labels")
plt.ylabel("Count")
plt.xticks(rotation=90)
plt.show()

EDA: Class Distribution
In [12]:
# print("EDA: Sample Images")
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 10))
for i in range(9):  # Display 9 sample images
    plt.subplot(3, 3, i + 1)
    img = Image.open(image_paths[i])
    plt.imshow(img)
    plt.title(f"Label: {labels[i]}")
    plt.axis("off")
plt.show()

Split the Data into Training, Validation, and Test Sets¶

In [13]:
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(image_paths, labels_encoded, test_size=0.2, random_state=42)
X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(X_train, y_train, test_size=0.2, random_state=42)

Define the Input Size for EfficientNet¶

In [14]:
input_size = (224, 224)

Function to Load and Preprocess an Image¶

In [15]:
def load_and_preprocess_image(image_path, label):
    image = tf.io.read_file(image_path)
    image = tf.image.decode_jpeg(image, channels=3)
    image = tf.image.resize(image, input_size)
    image = tf.keras.applications.efficientnet.preprocess_input(image)
    return image, label

Create TensorFlow Datasets with Augmentation¶

In [16]:
def load_and_preprocess_image(image_path, label):
    image = tf.io.read_file(image_path)
    image = tf.image.decode_jpeg(image, channels=3)
    image = tf.image.resize(image, input_size)
    image = tf.keras.applications.efficientnet.preprocess_input(image)
    return image, label

train_dataset = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices((X_train, y_train))
train_dataset = train_dataset.map(load_and_preprocess_image).batch(32).prefetch(tf.data.AUTOTUNE)

val_dataset = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices((X_val, y_val))
val_dataset = val_dataset.map(load_and_preprocess_image).batch(32).prefetch(tf.data.AUTOTUNE)

test_dataset = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices((X_test, y_test))
test_dataset = test_dataset.map(load_and_preprocess_image).batch(32).prefetch(tf.data.AUTOTUNE)

Load Pre-trained EfficientNetB0 Model¶

In [17]:
base_model = EfficientNetB0(weights='imagenet', include_top=False, input_shape=(224, 224, 3))

# Freeze the base model layers
base_model.trainable = False

Add Custom Classification Head¶

In [18]:
x = base_model.output
x = GlobalAveragePooling2D()(x)
x = Dense(1024, activation='relu')(x)
x = Dropout(0.5)(x)  # Add dropout to reduce overfitting
predictions = Dense(num_classes, activation='softmax')(x)

# Create the final model
model = Model(inputs=base_model.input, outputs=predictions)

Compile the Model for Initial Training¶

In [19]:
model.compile(optimizer=Adam(learning_rate=0.001), loss='sparse_categorical_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])

Train the Model¶

In [20]:
# Train the Model (Initial Training Phase)
print("Initial Training Phase: Training the custom head with the base model frozen...")
early_stopping = EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=5, restore_best_weights=True)
reduce_lr = ReduceLROnPlateau(monitor='val_loss', factor=0.2, patience=3, min_lr=1e-6)

history_initial = model.fit(
    train_dataset,
    validation_data=val_dataset,
    epochs=20,
    callbacks=[early_stopping, reduce_lr]
)

Initial Training Phase: Training the custom head with the base model frozen...

In [22]:
# Evaluate the Model on the Test Set
print("Evaluating the model on the test set...")
test_loss, test_accuracy = model.evaluate(test_dataset)
print(f"Test Loss: {test_loss:.4f}")
print(f"Test Accuracy: {test_accuracy * 100:.2f}%")

# Evaluate the Model on the Train Set
print("Evaluating the model on the train set...")
train_loss, train_accuracy = model.evaluate(train_dataset)
print(f"Train Loss: {train_loss:.4f}")
print(f"Train Accuracy: {train_accuracy * 100:.2f}%")

# Compute Predictions on the Test Set
y_pred_test = model.predict(test_dataset)
y_pred_test_classes = np.argmax(y_pred_test, axis=1)  # Convert probabilities to class labels

# Compute Predictions on the Train Set
y_pred_train = model.predict(train_dataset)
y_pred_train_classes = np.argmax(y_pred_train, axis=1)  # Convert probabilities to class labels

Evaluating the model on the test set...
125/125 [==============================] - 161s 1s/step - loss: 0.1201 - accuracy: 0.9610
Test Loss: 0.1201
Test Accuracy: 96.10%
Evaluating the model on the train set...
400/400 [==============================] - 545s 1s/step - loss: 0.0826 - accuracy: 0.9712
Train Loss: 0.0826
Train Accuracy: 97.12%
125/125 [==============================] - 155s 1s/step
400/400 [==============================] - 475s 1s/step
In [23]:
# compute Confusion Matrix for Test Set
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix
conf_matrix_test = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred_test_classes)
print("Confusion Matrix (Test Set):")
print(conf_matrix_test)

# Compute Confusion Matrix for Train Set
conf_matrix_train = confusion_matrix(y_train, y_pred_train_classes)
print("Confusion Matrix (Train Set):")
print(conf_matrix_train)

#  Compute AUC for Test Set (One-vs-Rest for Multi-Class)
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
auc_test = roc_auc_score(y_test, y_pred_test, multi_class='ovr')
print(f"AUC (Test Set): {auc_test:.4f}")

# Compute AUC for Train Set (One-vs-Rest for Multi-Class)
auc_train = roc_auc_score(y_train, y_pred_train, multi_class='ovr')
print(f"AUC (Train Set): {auc_train:.4f}")

# Plot ROC Curve for Test Set
from sklearn.metrics import RocCurveDisplay
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Plot ROC curve for each class (Test Set)
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
for i in range(num_classes):
    RocCurveDisplay.from_predictions(
        y_test == i, y_pred_test[:, i],
        name=f"Class {i} vs Rest (Test Set)",
        ax=plt.gca()
    )
plt.title("ROC Curve (Test Set)")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.show()

#  Plot ROC Curve for Train Set
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
for i in range(num_classes):
    RocCurveDisplay.from_predictions(
        y_train == i, y_pred_train[:, i],
        name=f"Class {i} vs Rest (Train Set)",
        ax=plt.gca()
    )
plt.title("ROC Curve (Train Set)")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.show()

# Classification Report for Test Set
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
print("Classification Report (Test Set):")
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred_test_classes, target_names=label_encoder.classes_))

# Classification Report for Train Set
print("Classification Report (Train Set):")
print(classification_report(y_train, y_pred_train_classes, target_names=label_encoder.classes_))

Fine-Tuning Phase¶

In [24]:
print("Fine-Tuning Phase: Unfreezing some layers and training with a lower learning rate...")

# Unfreeze the top 50 layers of the base model
base_model.trainable = True
for layer in base_model.layers[:-50]:
    layer.trainable = False

# Recompile the model with a lower learning rate
model.compile(optimizer=Adam(learning_rate=1e-5), loss='sparse_categorical_crossentropy', metrics=['accuracy'])

# Train the model with fine-tuning
history_fine = model.fit(
    train_dataset,
    validation_data=val_dataset,
    epochs=4,
    callbacks=[early_stopping, reduce_lr]
)

Fine-Tuning Phase: Unfreezing some layers and training with a lower learning rate...

Model Evaluation after fine tuning on the Test and train Set¶

In [25]:
# Evaluate the Model on the Test Set
print("Evaluating the model on the test set...")
test_loss, test_accuracy = model.evaluate(test_dataset)
print(f"Test Loss: {test_loss:.4f}")
print(f"Test Accuracy: {test_accuracy * 100:.2f}%")

# Evaluate the Model on the Train Set
print("Evaluating the model on the train set...")
train_loss, train_accuracy = model.evaluate(train_dataset)
print(f"Train Loss: {train_loss:.4f}")
print(f"Train Accuracy: {train_accuracy * 100:.2f}%")

# Compute Predictions on the Test Set
y_pred_test = model.predict(test_dataset)
y_pred_test_classes = np.argmax(y_pred_test, axis=1)  # Convert probabilities to class labels

# Compute Predictions on the Train Set
y_pred_train = model.predict(train_dataset)
y_pred_train_classes = np.argmax(y_pred_train, axis=1)  # Convert probabilities to class labels

Compute Confusion Matrix and AUC¶

In [26]:
# compute Confusion Matrix for Test Set
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix
conf_matrix_test = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred_test_classes)
print("Confusion Matrix (Test Set):")
print(conf_matrix_test)

# Compute Confusion Matrix for Train Set
conf_matrix_train = confusion_matrix(y_train, y_pred_train_classes)
print("Confusion Matrix (Train Set):")
print(conf_matrix_train)

#  Compute AUC for Test Set (One-vs-Rest for Multi-Class)
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
auc_test = roc_auc_score(y_test, y_pred_test, multi_class='ovr')
print(f"AUC (Test Set): {auc_test:.4f}")

# Compute AUC for Train Set (One-vs-Rest for Multi-Class)
auc_train = roc_auc_score(y_train, y_pred_train, multi_class='ovr')
print(f"AUC (Train Set): {auc_train:.4f}")

# Plot ROC Curve for Test Set
from sklearn.metrics import RocCurveDisplay
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Plot ROC curve for each class (Test Set)
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
for i in range(num_classes):
    RocCurveDisplay.from_predictions(
        y_test == i, y_pred_test[:, i],
        name=f"Class {i} vs Rest (Test Set)",
        ax=plt.gca()
    )
plt.title("ROC Curve (Test Set)")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.show()

#  Plot ROC Curve for Train Set
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
for i in range(num_classes):
    RocCurveDisplay.from_predictions(
        y_train == i, y_pred_train[:, i],
        name=f"Class {i} vs Rest (Train Set)",
        ax=plt.gca()
    )
plt.title("ROC Curve (Train Set)")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.show()

# Classification Report for Test Set
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
print("Classification Report (Test Set):")
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred_test_classes, target_names=label_encoder.classes_))

# Classification Report for Train Set
print("Classification Report (Train Set):")
print(classification_report(y_train, y_pred_train_classes, target_names=label_encoder.classes_))

    accuracy                           0.98     12800
   macro avg       0.96      0.97      0.97     12800
weighted avg       0.98      0.98      0.98     12800

Model Performance:¶

The model performs very well on both the train set and test set, as indicated by the high AUC scores.

The confusion matrices show that most predictions are correct, with relatively few misclassification s.

Overfitting:

The train AUC (0.9985) is slightly higher than the test AUC (0.9968), which suggests that the model may be slightly overfitting to the training data.

However, the difference is small, so the overfitting is minimal.

Class-Specific Performance:

Some classes (e.g., Class 1 in the test set) have very high accuracy, while others (e.g., Class 0 in the test set) have more misclassifications.

This could indicate that certain classes are harder for the model to distinguish.

Visualising our predictions vs actual label on the test set¶

In [30]:
#Define a Function to Visualize Predictions
def visualize_predictions(test_dataset, model, label_encoder, num_images=5):
    # Take a batch of images and labels from the test dataset
    for images, labels in test_dataset.take(1):
        # Make predictions
        predictions = model.predict(images)
        predicted_classes = np.argmax(predictions, axis=1)  # Get predicted class indices
        
        # Decode the actual and predicted labels
        actual_labels = label_encoder.inverse_transform(labels.numpy())
        predicted_labels = label_encoder.inverse_transform(predicted_classes)
        
        # Plot the images with actual and predicted labels
        plt.figure(figsize=(15, 10))
        for i in range(min(num_images, len(images))):  # Limit to `num_images`
            plt.subplot(1, num_images, i + 1)
            plt.imshow(images[i].numpy().astype('uint8'))  # Display the image
            plt.title(f"Actual: {actual_labels[i]}\nPredicted: {predicted_labels[i]}", fontsize=10)
            plt.axis('off')
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()

print("\nVisualizing predictions on test set images...")
visualize_predictions(test_dataset, model, label_encoder, num_images=10)  

Visualizing predictions on test set images...
